qa_evidence.duplicate: No duplicate IDs, normalized titles, canonical websites or media IDs detected.

records.title: Check whether you need a housing permit
records.description: Some municipalities require a housing permit before a person can occupy a social rental home. The municipality decides whether a permit is used and sets local conditions concerning income, household size or regional connection.

official_source.title: Heb ik een huisvestingsvergunning nodig om in een sociale huurwoning te wonen?

records.ai_summary: Check the rules of the municipality where the social rental home is located. If a housing permit is required, apply to that municipality and verify its current income, household and local-connection conditions.

attributes.scope: Local rules for social rental housing

records.title: Understand municipal taxes and charges
records.description: Municipalities may levy taxes and service charges authorised by law, including property tax, waste and sewer charges, parking tax and fees. The municipal council determines local rates each year, so amounts differ by municipality.
records.ai_summary: Consult the municipality for the taxes, charges and current rates that apply locally. A resident may challenge an assessment or valuation in qualifying circumstances, but local tax types and amounts are not uniform nationwide.

records.title: Report a crime to the Netherlands Police
records.description: Victims can report a crime to the Netherlands Police through the route matching their situation. The available process differs for residents, visitors and people abroad who were affected by a Dutch person or company.
records.ai_summary: Use the official Police reporting route that matches where you live or stay. Call 112 when urgent help is required; for non-emergency police assistance use the official general number or the reporting options shown by Police.

records.title: Check eligibility for subsidised legal aid
records.description: Judicial proceedings can involve court, lawyer, bailiff, mediator and expert costs. In qualifying cases the government contributes to court fees and the cost of assigned legal counsel or mediation, depending on income and assets.
records.ai_summary: Estimate the costs of the relevant type of proceedings and check whether subsidised legal aid applies. Eligibility and the personal contribution depend on income, assets and the case; costs awarded to the other party are not covered.

records.title: Call emergency number 112
records.description: The emergency number 112 is for urgent or potentially life-threatening situations, serious injury and serious crimes in progress. The operator asks what happened, what help is needed and whether police, fire or ambulance assistance is required.
records.ai_summary: Call 112 when every second counts or a situation presents immediate serious danger. Tell the operator what happened, the help needed and the relevant emergency service; use non-emergency channels when no urgent danger remains.

records.title: Respond to an NL-Alert warning
records.description: NL-Alert is the Dutch public warning system for harmful and life-threatening situations such as major fires, severe weather, epidemics or attacks. Alerts explain the danger and the action people in the affected area should take.
records.ai_summary: Read an NL-Alert immediately, follow its instructions and help others nearby. Alerts use cell broadcast, are free and anonymous, require no registration and can also appear on public digital signs.

records.title: Vote in Dutch municipal elections
records.description: Eligible residents vote for their municipal council using a voting pass and accepted identity document. Eligibility depends on age, registration in the Personal Records Database and nationality or qualifying lawful residence.

official_source.title: What do I need to vote in the municipal elections?

records.ai_summary: Before an election, verify the current eligibility rules and polling information. Bring the voting pass and an accepted identity document; residents who cannot vote personally may be able to authorise another voter under the proxy rules.

attributes.required_documents: Voting pass and accepted identity document

records.title: How Dutch parliamentary democracy works
records.description: The Netherlands is a parliamentary democracy in which people elect representatives to make decisions on their behalf. Representation operates nationally and through provincial, municipal and water-authority government.
records.ai_summary: Dutch voters elect representatives who scrutinise government and make public decisions. Democratic representation exists at national, provincial, municipal and water-authority levels, with responsibilities divided across those levels.

records.title: Report a consumer problem to ACM ConsuWijzer
records.description: Consumers can report a company they believe is breaking consumer rules to ACM ConsuWijzer. Reports help the regulator identify patterns and decide where to intervene, although an individual report does not always lead to an investigation.

official_source.title: Doe uw melding bij ACM ConsuWijzer

records.ai_summary: Try to resolve the problem with the company and retain supporting evidence. Report suspected rule-breaking to ACM ConsuWijzer; the regulator uses reports for supervision but may not provide an individual follow-up unless more information is needed.

records.title: Exercise your privacy rights under the GDPR
records.description: People have rights over personal data used by organisations, including information, access, rectification, erasure and data portability. If an organisation refuses a valid request, a complaint can be filed with the Dutch Data Protection Authority or the matter can go to court.
records.ai_summary: Send the organisation a clear written request identifying the privacy right being exercised and keep a copy. If it does not respond properly, contact its privacy officer and consider a complaint to the Dutch Data Protection Authority.

records.title: Arrange official matters after a death
records.description: After a death, the next of kin may need to arrange registration of the death, the funeral and administrative matters involving organisations, accounts, benefits and contracts. Which steps apply depends on the deceased person's circumstances.

official_source.title: After a death: what do I need to arrange?

records.ai_summary: Start with the official death registration and funeral arrangements, then identify the organisations, benefits, accounts and contracts that need attention. Use the government checklist to tailor the steps to the deceased person's situation.

records.title: Choose whether to accept an inheritance
records.description: An heir can accept an inheritance outright, accept it under benefit of inventory or refuse it. The choice affects responsibility for debts, and accepting under benefit of inventory or refusing requires a declaration at the district court.

official_source.title: Do I have to accept an inheritance?

records.ai_summary: Before acting on estate assets, determine whether the estate may contain debts and choose the appropriate form of acceptance or refusal. Benefit-of-inventory acceptance and refusal require a district-court declaration and can involve costs.

attributes.available_choices: Outright acceptance, benefit of inventory or refusal
